calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Accounting
REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Accounting
Off Calendar May-04-2026 Continued To Jul-15-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Nicholas Paul Jellins, Esq. for and with Administrator Donovan Phillips;
Gabrielle Gomez, Esq. for Assignee Inheritance Funding Company.

The Court stated that the matter was on calendar for status of the accounting and that at the last call of the matter there was no waiver of the accounting. Ms. Gomez confirmed that the accounting was not waived. Mr. Jellins stated that he had spoken with Eric Forsberg at Inheritance Funding Company and that Mr. Forsberg gave him additional time. Ms. Gomez confirmed that Mr. Jellins was given additional time, and briefly addressed the real property left by Lovie May's predeceased spouse noting that the assest will flow to Lovie May's estate. Mr. Jellins addresses the spousal election, the trickle down to the same heirs, and heirs receiving one third of the assets. It was noted that the predeceased spousal petition is on calendar tomorrow and is going to be continued.

The Court continued the matter to July 15, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. A status report is to be filed five days before the hearing.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Guitron. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Apr-13-2026 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Accounting
Off Calendar Mar-30-2026 Continued To May-04-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nicholas Jellins for administrator Donovan Phillips, who was also present.
Gabrielle Gomez for assignee Inheritance Funding Company.

The matter was called for hearing on Status on Accounting.

Mr. Jellins reported that no funds remain in the estate and that the administrator is exploring potential assets through a probate of the decedent's predeceased spouse's estate in order to fund a distribution to Inheritance Funding Company.

The Court addressed the need for an accounting and stated it would not be waived absent agreement by Inheritance Funding Company. Counsel for Inheritance Funding Company stated they are awaiting a proposal regarding payment of their interest.

Mr. Phillips stated he had been in touch with the VP of Inheritance Funding Company and requested additional time to meet and confer regarding the accounting. The Court encouraged further discussions but stated that an accounting would be required absent a waiver.

The matter was continued to May 4, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 for status of accounting. The Court directed that an accounting be filed or a waiver obtained prior to the continued hearing.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Accounting
Off Calendar Jan-26-2026 Continued To Mar-30-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nicholas Jellins for administrator Donovan Phillips, who was also present.

The matter was called for hearing on status on accounting.

Mr. Jellins reported that, at a prior hearing, the Court ordered an accounting after estate funds were distributed without prior court order. Mr. Jellins stated that the heirs had waived an accounting, but that Inheritance Funding Company had not.

The Court stated that, because Inheritance Funding Company stood in the position of a beneficiary and had not waived an accounting, a waiver of accounting could not be accepted. The Court stated that the appropriate procedural step was to file a petition to settle a first interim account.

The Court continued the matter to March 30, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-15-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nicholas P. Jellins for administrator Donovan Phillips, who was also present.
Gabrielle Gomez for interested party Inheritance Funding Company.

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Settlement of First and Final Account and Final Distribution.

Mr. Jellins reported that after the petition was filed, it was discovered that estate funds had already been distributed to heirs without prior court authorization and that the estate no longer held sufficient liquid assets to complete the proposed distribution. He further reported that the administrator had identified additional potential estate assets consisting of multiple parcels of real property in Lassen County, but that ownership was unclear and would require further investigation.

Ms. Gomez objected to the prior distributions having been made without her client's involvement and requested an accounting to determine what funds were received by the estate and how those funds were distributed. The Court noted that, given the unresolved asset issues and uncertainty regarding prior distributions, the petition in its current form could not proceed.

Following discussion, the Court took the petition off calendar and ordered the administrator to file an updated accounting of all estate receipts and disbursements. A status hearing regarding the accounting was set for January 26, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Court also set a hearing for status on close of estate for April 13, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-05-2025 Continued To Jun-02-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Nicholas Jellins appeared via zoom for and with Donovan Phillips (petitioner).

The Court issues sanctions in the amount of $250.00 against Nicholas Jellins Pursuant to CCP 128, payable within 10 days of the order. Mr. Jellins stated that he filed the inventory and appraisal. Status on close of estate is continued to June 2, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Thompson. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-10-2025 Continued To May-05-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The court shall issue an Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause for attorney Nicholas P. Jellins to appear on May 5, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to explain why sanctions in the amount of $250 should not be imposed for his failure to appear. An Order to Appear shall also be issued to administrator Donovan Phillips to appear on the same date.

The court continued the matter to May 5, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate

Appearances:
Nicholas Jellins appeared via zoom for Donvan Phillips (adminstrator).

Mr. Jellins stated that he is 90% complete with the petition and is seeking a continuance. The matter is continued to March 10, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-06-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nicholas Jellins for Donavan Phillips, Petitioner/Administrator (via Zoom).

Mr. Jellins reported that the estate is ready to file a petition for final distribution with a waiver of accounting. Delayed bank records have now been obtained.

The hearing on report of status of administration was taken off calendar. A status on close of estate hearing was set for February 3, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, pending the filing of the petition for final distribution.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
